series_description: All files pertaining to complaints, petitions, writs, applications and appeals filed in federal district court, a federal court of appeals or the U.S. Supreme court where the Department and/or departmental employees are parties to the lawsuit.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years after case is decided and appeals are exhausted

series_title: Prison Nursery Program/Infants Born During Confine
series_description: All files pertaining to children born during mother''s incarceration. This could include children who reside with their mother for any period of mother''s incarceration.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 21 years after child''s birth ; Electronic: Retain 21 years after child''s birth

series_description: Ohio court cases against the Department and/or departmental employees filed in Ohio Courts other than the Court of Claims. This category includes any complaint, writ, petition, application or appeal that may be heard by a municipal court, court of common pleas, court of appeals, and the Ohio Supreme Court. All files pertaining to any action filed in a division of an Ohio municipal court, court of common pleas, district court of appeals or Ohio Supreme Court.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years after case is decided and appeals are exhausted

series_description: All records pertaining to the inmate that are kept in the unit where the inmate resides.
retention_statement: Paper: This file will be merged with inmate master file upon inmate''s release from custody. Retain 10 years after final release, maximum expiration, expiration of sentence, death, etc.

series_description: All files pertaining to complaints filed in the Court of Claims (administrative and judicial dockets) against the department or a state correctional institution.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years after case is decided and appeals are exhausted

series_description: Recovery Services Record to include all documentation related to substance abuse evaluation and treatment services provided to an offender while incarcerated within the Department of Rehabilitation and Correction.
retention_statement: Paper: Merge with inmate master file upon release. Retain for ten years following final release, death, or maximum expiration of sentence.

series_description: Proceedings, records, findings, recommendations, evaluations, minutes, reports, opinions and other documents that emanate from quality assurance committees, quality assurance programs, or quality assurance program activities.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years

series_description: Mental Health Record to include all clinical documentation related to the diagnosis and treatment provided to an offender while incarcerated within the Department of Rehabilitation and Correction
retention_statement: Paper: Mental Health Record is maintained at the institution while offender is incarcerated. When offender is released, record is to be maintained in storage for 10 years.

series_description: Includes duplicate case records for Ohio parole and probation cases. Contains face sheet, parole plan summary, Federal Bureau of Investigation and BCI reports, institution reports, psychological, medical, education and discipline reports (copies); authorization of release to parole, reports of parole progress and special information and general correspondence. Evidence of final release from parole supervision and Interstate Compact correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: 2 years after final release from parole supervison

series_description: Contains records of parole and probation cases of offenders who transfer to Ohio from other states for supervision. The sending state retains original file. The compact file contains face sheet information and investigation reports; reports of parole or probation progress and copies of authorization of release from supervision as they are received from the sending state. Contain medical, psychological, social summaries and general correspondence relating to the supervision of the parolee or probationer.
retention_statement: Paper: Files of parolees and probationers released form supervision. Kept in paper form and not microfilmed. Retained for 2 years after final release from suoervision

series_description: Includes treatment records and patient history. It contains all legal, administrative, treatment and other information relative to the entry, treatment and discharge of any inmate treated in the Department of Rehabilitation and Correction. It also contains most of the records listed in the Standard Medical Record Manual. It includes the contents of the Clinic Treatment Records.
retention_statement: Paper: This file will be merged with inmate master file upon inmates release from custody. Retain for ten (10) years after final release, maximum expiration of sentance, death, definite expiration, etc.

series_description: Includes all mail related records including mail cards which contain names and addresses of all correspondents and authorization for special letters.
retention_statement: Paper: This file will be merged with inmate master file upon inmates release from custody. Retain for ten (10) years after final release, maximum expiration, death, etc.

series_description: A file that contains documentation regarding an incarcerated person's visitors and visiting record.
retention_statement: Paper: This file will be merged with the incarcerated person master file upon incarcerated person's release from custody. Retain for ten (10) years after final release, maximum expiration, death, definite expiration, etc. ; Electronic: This file will be merged with the incarcerated person master file upon incarcerated person's release from custody. Retain for ten (10) years after final release, maximum expiration, death, definite expiration, etc.

series_description: Log of each visitor who visits on each day. Lists inmate name and institution number.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for ten (10) years after final release, maximum expiration, death, definite expiration, etc.

series_description: All records of an institutional disciplinary nature including records that show rules infractions by inmates and institution and departmental decisions regarding discipline.
retention_statement: Paper: This file will be merged with inmate master file upon inmates release from custody. Retain for ten (10) years after final release, maximum expiration, death, definite expiration, etc.

series_description: Includes all social service records prepared as needed including social summaries and evaluations for routine operations, admissions and special requests.
retention_statement: Paper: This file will be merged with inmate master file upon inmates release from custody. Retain for ten (10) years after final release, maximum expiration, death, definite expiration, etc.

series_description: Alphabetical reference. Contains name of inmate, age, date entered institution, county sentanced; date paroled or otherwise released from the institution; date of release from parole supervision.
retention_statement: Microform: Retain for 75 years ; Paper: Cards will be microfilmed 10 years after final release, maximum expiration, death, etc. Paper copy to be destroyed after microfilm checked for quality and completeness

series_description: Record checks received from the LEADS computerized communication network.
retention_statement: Paper: Inmate record checks should be maintained in the inmate master file. All other record checks should be retained until the review is complete.

series_description: Notes resulting from investigations, interviews and/or correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until investigation/interview is complete

series_description: DRC Form #2003 that authorizes transfer of inmates from one institution to another.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until transfer is complete ; Electronic: Retain in office until transfer is complete retain for ten years following the offender''''''''s final release or maximum expiration of sentence.

series_description: Drawings, specifications of institutional generators, and operator/maintenance manuals.
retention_statement: Paper: Retained until generator is salvaged

series_description: Detailed Goals and Odjectives of the department with each division/section responsibilities.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until New Goals and Objectives are stated

series_description: Architectural drawings, specifications and pre-design planning documents for the construction or renovation of Department facilities/buildings.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintained until building destroyed

series_description: Firearms authorizations for personal and state issued weapons for parole and probation officers of the Adult Parole Authority. These records include training records.
retention_statement: Paper: Authorization form is maintained in Personnel File for seven years

series_description: Documents officer assigned at each post for each shift.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office three years

series_description: Repair history on each piece of equipment in the institution.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until equipment is replaced or salvaged

series_description: Blueprints of all institutional buildings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retained until building or facility destroyed

series_description: Log of medication being brought into the institution for inmate personal needs during the time spent confined in the institution.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 6 months

series_description: Document utilized to conduct daily institutional count.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Documents searches made and lists of contraband found during the search.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year from date of search

series_description: Documents specifies on assaults on staff by inmates and includes yearly statistical report on assaults.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office one year

series_description: Documents process of offenders marriage during incarceration. Paper files with court documents to include: kites, letters of request to get married, reply of approval or denial, scheduling notice, copy of marriage license, change of name memos, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office three years after maximum expiration, final release or death

series_description: Documents every person entering institution (signature, from, to see whom). (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years

series_description: Monthly statistical reports that document the activities of the divisions, bureaus, and/or institutions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years.

series_title: Rules and Infractions Board Deductions L
series_description: Documents deduction from inmates state pay to reimburse the state for destruction of state property.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until funds repaid and audit completed and released

series_description: Records monthly commission to be credited to inmates account. Commission for Industrial Arts Sales.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_description: Log of all money orders received in the mailroom to be credited to inmate accounts. Includes copies of all money orders.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 4 years and destroy provided audit completed and report released.

series_description: Records stock issued/received and balance on hand.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_description: Records amount of food used in preparation of institution meals and monthly usage totals.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: (Minutes) Documents subjects discussed and decisions made at committee meetings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until a new contract is negotiated

series_description: Documents the end in the hiring process for each position posted and filled. File includes application, score sheets, interview questions, selections, etc.)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years after file is closed (unless file is in litigation)

series_description: A log that document badges issued to persons entering the institution.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years

series_description: Quarterly list of institution employees with name, social security number, classification, institution seniority, agency seniority.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain by Labor Relations office for five years

series_description: An organizational chart that illustrates the chain of commands within the institutions, divisions, and bureaus of the Ohio Department of Rehabilitation and Correction.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 years ; Electronic: 3 years

series_description: Documents process used to equalized overtime opportunities. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office one year

series_description: Sign-up record for officers volunteering for overtime. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until end of quarter

series_description: Status report on vacant positions (posted, held, created). (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for three years

series_description: Request for Central Office to post position for hire at institutions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for one year after the audit report is released.

series_description: Documents removal of probationary employees and reason for removal. (Statistical information, date of hire, date evaluated, final probationary evaluation). (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two years in office

series_description: Description of inmate jobs while working in the Ohio Penal Industries.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office three years following reclass act of OPI

series_description: Marketing information used to create sales brochures and product catalogues.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Documents produced either by Syteline or manually that show what products have been shipped from the shop to the central distribution center or directly to customer.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Records provide monthly fiscal overview including profit and loss of each shop within Ohio Penal Industries.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: A file for each incarcerated person of inventoried and/or titled property as defined in DRC Policy 61-PRP-01.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for ten (10) years after incarcerated person's final release, maximum expiration, death, definite expiration, etc. ; Electronic: Retain for ten (10) years after incarcerated person's final release, maximum expiration, death, definite expiration, etc.

series_description: Logs date the inmates RIB must be heard by, date actually heard, disposition received, date to be released by, and disciplinary act.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three years or until the end of current ACA Accreditation period.

series_description: Documents any cell or bed changes within a Unit. (Samples taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office one month

series_description: Notification of APA to inmates to appear before the Parole Board. APA = Adult Parole Authority (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office six months

series_description: Documents officers call ins from post at required times. (Officers required to report in from their post at certain times).
retention_statement: Paper: In office 1 year

series_description: Documents total count of the entire institution taken at each count time (8 times a day).
retention_statement: Paper: In office 1 year

series_description: Documents institution locks inspected daily (3 times a day) by each shift.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 month

series_description: Documents each alert of electronic fence and who checked it out and disposition of alert.
retention_statement: Paper: In office 6 months

series_description: Documents theft of inmate property and the disposition of the investigation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years.

series_description: Documents security rounds made and what areas were checked. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year after date of inspection

series_description: Documents authorization to change a key on a key ring within the institution. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years after change

series_description: Complete information (background investigation, questionnaire, signed notification of rules and regulations) on volunteer.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in Training Office at facility of original volunteer site. When volunteer services have ended/terminated, retain for three years

series_description: Surveys, Pay request information, specifications, drawings and purchase order information on electrical Projects. (Active & completed).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 years

series_description: Record of Fire Extinguisher Inspection. DRC Form #1209-Fire Extinguisher Inspection Report.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle) ; Electronic: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Records action taken to abate OSHA Violation resulting from OSHA audit. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Maintained until violations are taken care of and a clear OSHA Audit is obtained

series_description: Records inmates monthly payroll.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until one year after pay date.

series_description: Notification of all inmate moved within the institution.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain six months in office

series_description: Generated reports from TIE that records and notifies institution job coordinators and unit staff of new offenders that need to be classified.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office one year

series_description: Documents entire investigation process, including sexual abuse investigations, describing the facts found while conducting an investigation involving an employee or offender''s alleged violation of policy, procedure, or Ohio Revised Code
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 10 years after inmate has reached final release, expiration of sentence, death, or 10 years after employee is no longer employed by the agency. ; Electronic: Retain 10 years after inmate has reached final release, expiration of sentence, death, or 10 years after employee is no longer employed by the agency.

series_description: Upon request by the inmate, guilty findings of certain rule violations by the Rules Infraction Board must be reviewed by the Director or designee. Record includes entire case file and Director''s decision.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office two years

series_description: Title and Registrations, Warranty, Guarantee statements and operating manuals of all department vehicles.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until vehicle is salvaged

series_description: Reports on energy use in institutions, (gas, electric, etc.) used to project future utility needs.
retention_statement: Paper: In office 5 years. In records center 20 years

series_description: Underground storage tank reports on location, inspection and maintenance. These tanks may contain fuel or other chemicals.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until underground tanks are removed

series_description: Documents packages sent out by inmates. Used to verify amount charged to inmates account and to verify that the package was sent.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years.

series_description: Documents sales tax paid due to items being sold at the institutions. Keeps track of sales tax generated from sale of goods made by inmates to determine amount paid to Department of Taxation.
retention_statement: Paper: In office retain until audited and report released

series_description: Documents checks returned (purchases) to be credited back to inmates accounts. When a check was returned from vendor because item was discontinued or out-of-stock, this documents that the amount was credited back to inmates account.
retention_statement: Paper: In office retain one year

series_description: Documents checks for refunds ( tax, purchase returns) being credited to inmate account.
retention_statement: Paper: In office retain one year

series_description: Documents the total amount of funds the inmate is released with.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for a minimum of 2 years and until audited with a released report.

series_description: Daily record of money receipts from funds left by visitors. Daily record to be credited to inmates account.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released. ; Electronic: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_description: Reports on House Bill Monies. Document breaks down how much money is spent and balance remaining for the construction and repair of Institution buildings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_description: Inmates monthly/daily average balance of inmate account to be filed with indigent claims to the court. If inmate can prove that his average six month balance falls below a certain amount he is declared indigent and is entitled to free legal assistance.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report released.

series_description: Individual itemized list of inmate commisary purchases. Used to verify that withdrawal from inmates accounts was correct.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_title: Cashier Office Back Up Computer Disk
series_description: End of month back up of computerized fiscal balances of individual inmate accounts.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_description: Information regarding bulk purchases and their disbursement. This is a record of bulk supplies purchased for all institution and how supplies were distributed.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_title: Cash Slips (Inmates Request to Send Checks)
series_description: Request from inmate to Cashiers Office to debit account and send out check. This slip is retained to verify the correct amount was taken out of inmate''s account.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_description: Institution reports on inmates incarcerated that are under the age of 21. The department is reimbursed for lunch meals consumed by inmates under the age of 21.
retention_statement: Paper: In office retain 3 years after end of calendar year.

series_description: Nutritionally balanced menus to be used by the institutions, published by Central Office.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintained until superceded

series_description: Records temperature for hot lines, deep fryers, and cold lines. This is to ensure cooking conditions are correct to prevent food poisoning.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Records inmate assignment in kitchen area. Additional record-keeping of who was issued utensils in kitchen.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (curent audit cycle)

series_description: Documents the results of random meal testing in the institution.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Special diet orders. These diets are issued on either medical or religious grounds. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until inmate transfers or is released

series_description: Record used to document items used in preparation of daily meals.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three years.

series_description: Documents the training each Food Manager has completed. This is used to verify that the employee has completed the necessary training to be qualified as Food Service Manager.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 10 years after employee leaves service.

series_description: Records employee disciplinary actions and date to remove actions from their files. (Samples taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until action is pulled from file

series_description: Documents employees call in absences (sick/personal leave). Calls are made to control room which notifies the appropriate supervisor. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year

series_description: Prescription for glasses produced at OPI Eyeglass shop. Glasses are produced for other inmates and patients of some state run institutions. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 7 years

series_description: Records number of items received in Laundry and number of items sent back. This log lists the number of items in bags received by the central laundry from each area and the number of items returned to insure that the correct number of items was returned
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 months

series_description: Records items and number received in laundry and number sent back. This log lists the items submitted to the area laundry bag by each inmate. Kept to verify that each inmate received all items he submitted to the laundry. (Sample taken at Ohio Reform
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 6 months

series_description: A log used to document the communication (kites) between inmate and staff.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 3 years

series_description: File excluding master files or satellite files as defined in DRC Policy 07-ORD-13 that contains copies of communication (kites) between inmates and staff.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until inmate is transfered or released from institution

series_description: Documents entire grievance process from grievance initiation to final administrative distribution and all related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain paper records 180 days after conversion to electronic format and verification that electronic images are clear and accurate. ; Electronic: Maintain six years after case is decided.

series_description: Copies of parole and furlough violator at large records that document when the offender was not available for supervision and when the offender was apprehended.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain on paper until scanned into OnBase. ; Electronic: Maintian for 10 years following final release, expiration of sentence, or death.

series_description: Documents that an offender''s security level has been reviewed and the result of the review. This document and its information is used to determine an offender''s housing assignment, job and programming assignments.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in offender''s unit file until the offender is transferred or released

series_description: Record that is utilized to document the physical transfer of offenders.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for three years.

series_description: Documents the total count and list of all inmate records maintained in the specific department/unit/bureau.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for one year

series_description: Documents boiler reading of chemicals added, safety reading, test results, meter reading, and water usage. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years

series_description: Reports gage readings, steam flow, gas readings, etc. Records are kept to monitor operating conditions of power house. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years

series_description: Records readings being taken by scheduled operators. Records retained to monitor performance of boilers. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office two years

series_description: Documents daily happenings on each shift. Lists all abnormal occurances or observations made during shifts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years.

series_description: Documents circumstances of a visitor search conducted. Indicates type of search conducted and the results of that search.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 10 years.

series_description: Documents publications coming into the institution that are challenged as to being a safety or security risk. Indicates the date that the publication was received, the grounds on which it was challenged and the ruling made.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 4 years

series_description: Documents security inspections made of listed areas.
retention_statement: Paper: In office 1 year

series_description: Documents extra officers assigned to posts during fog alert. (Heavy fog surrounding institution). Used to insure equal distribution of fog alert duty.
retention_statement: Paper: In office 1 year

series_description: Documents department heads entrance and exit times recorded by Tower (Tower observances and Logs). This list observances by the tower guards as to the arrival and departure times of exempt employees who are not required to use a time clock. These are us
retention_statement: Paper: In office 1 year

series_description: Records each key on each institution key ring. Tracking system for institution keys. (Monthly, Alpha, Numerical, Content, Crosskey). (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year

series_description: Authorization to drive to destinations listed. Documents arrival and departure times. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year from the date of authorization

series_description: Documents major contraband (where and when found). (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year after case settled

series_description: Documents inventory of institution keys, camera, VCR and monitor and keys required to operate. (Completed on each shift) Form DRC 2250
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Documents inmates being transported to departmental medical facility and the reason. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office one year

series_description: Documents inmate's name and number to be seen by a doctor. (Also reason and time of appointment) (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Documents inmates to be transported to medical facility. Used to protect officers needed on transportation duty. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year

series_description: Log of all inmates having urine test done.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office three years

series_description: Authorization for inmate bed transfer.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one week after entered in computer. ; Electronic: Retain during offender''s incarceration.

series_description: Records that provide institutional staff the authority to screen inmate''s mail.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 10 years.

series_title: Monthly Report of Cash Book Balances & B
series_description: Documents instiutions monthly cash book balances and bank reconciliations, for inmates accounts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 3 years after audit by DRC internal audit staff

series_description: Nutrition and Dietetics training classes attended in class format. List of all inmates who attended.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 10 years

series_description: Records special dietary needs of inmates and shows if they actually were served a meal or not. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 10 years

series_description: Records provide documentation for institution and correctional industries American Correctional Association audits.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Electronic recordings of Parole, Post Release Control, Transitional Control Violation Sanction Process (VSP), and Kellogg Mitigation Hearings.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office two years from date of hearing

series_description: Inmates request to Governor to intervene on inmates behalf for sentance reductions.
retention_statement: Paper: Original Clemancy request when returned from the Governor''''s Office will be forwarded directly to the Bureau of Records to be scanned into OnBase. ; Electronic: Retain 75 years.

series_description: Written ledger of inmates admitted to ODRC before electronic data was captured. This register may contain dates, county of commitment, crime and sentence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain permanently.

series_description: Records passes issued to inmates. List of each pass and who they were assigned to. Used to check the validity of pass. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year

series_description: DRC Form #2014 that is utilized to document the transfer of offender records.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for three years.

series_description: Department Building/Facility construction, renovation, maintenance and EPA required improvement projects in progress and completed.
retention_statement: Paper: In office Retained until building demolished

series_description: Receipts for items sold at institutions (arts and crafts, photos). A general receipt is completed for day''s total receipts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_title: Return or Held Cash Item Report
series_description: Documents funds received for inmates where source questioned.
retention_statement: Paper: In office Retain until audited and report is released and funds released by probated.

series_description: Financial records for state subsidized halfway houses.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_description: Worksheet used to verify daily balance off computer.
retention_statement: Paper: In office Retain one year

series_description: Pay requests for any facility maintenance/ renovation done at institution.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_description: History of inmate''s financial transaction while incarcerated. Record of all deposits and withdrawls to inmate''s account.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_description: Statistical report on numbers of grievances filed.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years.

series_description: Requests for temporary assistance from temporary agencies. Request could be for clerical or other assistance.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain through a state and internal fiscal audit

series_description: Files that contain staff grievance documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year in office and

series_description: Discrimination complaint files. File contains: original history complaint form, case log, investigative report, exhibit/list and copies of evidence, request for information, and disposition of case.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years after case is closed ; Electronic: Retain five years after case is closed

series_description: Record of employee training and staff development opportunities completed to include both mandatory as designated by policy and non-mandatory topics. Attendance rosters, agendas, curricula, course evaluations/test results and training certificates may be used to document completed training activities.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintained electronically and original file may be destroy immediately upon placement onto a server or other mechanism for computerized access. Maintain ten years after employee terminates service ; Paper: Maintain ten years after employee terminates service

series_description: Record of training and staff development opportunities completed to include mandatory training as designated by policy. Attendance roster, agenda, curricula, course evaluation/test results and training certificates may be used to document completed training activities.
retention_statement: Electronic: If electronically maintained, original files may be destroyed immediately upon placement onto a server or other mechanism for computerized access. Maintain five years ; Paper: Maintain five years

series_description: Documents employee grievances filed. Reference and past grievances & decisions. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Maintained until death or termination of employee

series_description: Includes documentation for exempt employees filing for appeal through Personnel Board of Review.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two years in office

series_description: Modular Furniture and drawings for layout installation. Drawing used to determine quality of parts necessary to install modular furniture.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years following completion of installation

series_description: Documentation identifies goals and objectives for correctional industries.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain 10 years

series_description: Documents necessary for transferring an inmate incaarcerated in one state to the temporary custody of another state for untried criminal charges.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in the Inmate Master File for ten years after death, final release or definite sentence expiration

series_description: These files contain massive data on every presentance investigation and post sentance investigation completed by an officer employed by the State of Ohio.
retention_statement: Electronic: In office, 5 years

series_description: Surveys and final reports on asbestos removal projects.
retention_statement: Paper: In office 5 years In Records Center 10 years

series_description: Records work requested to be done. I.E.; repairs, plugged toilets, electrical outlets, etc.. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retained until work is completed and logged

series_description: Record of Safety Meeting minutes. Not to document training of staff or inmates. DRC Form: Safety Meeting Record 6080.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three years (current audit cycle) ; Electronic: Retain for three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Report of safety and sanitation inspection of DRC facility locations. DRC Form(s):#1256 Monthly Food Service Sanitation Report, #1257 Fire/Safety/Sanitation Monthly Inspection Report, #1396 Fire/Safety/Sanitation - Weekly Inspection Report, #1495 Weekly Food Service Sanitation Report.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle) ; Electronic: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: General Specifications on renovation projects.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until facility/building is destroyed.

series_description: Log of all work completed, date requested and date completed. (Sample taken at the Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Records analysis of coal purchased by the institution. Test conducted to insure that coal meets EPA specifications. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 10 years.

series_description: Authorization to search a visitor or employee. Warden issues authorization when he believes there is probable cause. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until 10 years after the date of authorization.

series_description: Daily count of inmates. Out-counts are used when inmate is out of area.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Documents contractor's notification of institution's security guidelines. Any contractor doing business in an institution is required to sign a statement indicating that he understands the institution's security guidelines and that he will follow them du
retention_statement: Paper: Maintained until project is completed

series_description: A chronological list of all pending and closed investigations.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain at facility six years ; Electronic: Retain at facility six years

series_description: Documents inmates who receive credit toward their release date through productive program participation including attendance records.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until release of all inmates on the list. ; Electronic: Retain until release of all inmates on the list.

series_title: Urine Test Results that Indicate Illegal Drug Use
series_description: Documents results of inmate''s urine test that indicate illegal drug use.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 years.

series_description: Statistical data on program, program goals, any goals met, racial breakdown and overall assessment of the program. Reference source for future grants. Used to monitor effectiveness of program. (Sample taken at Ohio Reformatory for Women)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 10 years

series_description: Information on inmate recreation programs. Monitors participation of each inmate in programs.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 4 years.

series_description: Inmate appeals to Local Control Committee recommendations. These findings are appealed to Warden and/or Director.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain records for minimum of three years (current audit cycle)

series_title: Research Studies, Data Analysis, and Sup
series_description: This series contains raw data, statistical analysis, and other research information relevant to various DRC offices and divisions. Collectively, the series contains important studies and documents involving legislative impact analysis, parole and communi
retention_statement: Paper: Burn or shred five (5) years after policy is rescinded or replaced by a new policy.

series_description: Section 5120-131 of the Ohio Revised Code provides that the managing officer of an institution under the jurisdiction of the Department of Rehabilitation and Correction with the approval of the Director may establish a local institution fund designated as the I&E Fund.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_description: Copies of all personnel actions, benefit forms, application forms, and any other documents concerning the employee are contained in the file. Original records are kept by the Department of Administrative Services, Division of Personnel.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for the length of employment or until death. Retain off-site for seven years after the end of the calendar year in which employment was terminated

series_description: Dental record to include all clinical documentation related to diagnosis and treatment provided to an offender while incarcerated.
retention_statement: Paper: Dental record is maintained at the institution while offender is incarcerated. Upon release, record is to be maintained in storage for ten (10) years.

series_description: Documents for training that is sponsored by the Bureau for external agencies (generally jail administrators across the state). Documents may include: sign-in sheets, handouts, agendas, individual registrations, attendance summaries, correspondence to speakers and training sites.
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy individual registration forms immediately following the training. Retain registration summary for Five (5) years after the training. Retain one copy of other documents until no longer of administrtive value

series_description: All forms and letters related to the initial certification request of a jail and recertification of the jail.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain iniial certification documents until facility is no longer certified. Destroy original certification Five (5) years after decertification unless recertified. Retain all recertification documents for two (2) recertification cycles.

series_description: Documents annual jail inspection process from beginning to end. Annual Data Items (DRC 7001-7003); Annual Inspection Form (DRC 7046-7048); Annual Inspection Report; Pending Inspection Letter; Health and Fire Inspection Reports; Plan of action form/documentation/correspondence; all other ocumentation as it relates to the annual jail inspection period (may include policies & procedures, copies of logs, etc). Includes Temporary Holding Facility Self-Audit documentation and correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain Five (5) years in individual jail files at regional office and central office

series_description: Letters and other documentation submitted to initiate/plan/complete all phases of the jail construction/renovation planning and approval process. May include staffing analysis, policies and procedures, financial data (funding/projected operational costs), construction specifications, site visit forms, letters/memos detailing construction process. DOES NOT INCLUDE: Phase approval letters and Phase 3 Final approved blueprints.
retention_statement: Paper: Policies, procedures and blueprints prior to Final Phase 3 can be destroyed after One (1) year of the facility opening. Retain all other construction documentation/correspondence for Five (5) years after the facility opens in individual jail files at Regional and Central Office location

series_title: Jail Construction/Renovation Blueprints and Phase Approval Documentation
series_description: Documents detailing the major approval steps in the Planning and Approval Process, such as Phase approval letters (specifically Phase 3,4, and 5); final Phase 3 approved blueprint drawings with Phase 4 updates. Memos/letters for construction/renovation technical assistance that did not go through the formal planning and approval process.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain until building is destroyed. If no historical value

series_title: Jail Complaints and Critical Incident Reports
series_description: Correspondence and documents received as a result of a complaint about a jail. Critical Incident forms and documentation in regards to Fires, Escapes, Suicides, Deaths or other incidents in local jails as they are reported to the Bureau.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain Five (5) years in individual jail files at regional and central office location

series_title: Variance and Deviation Requests and B.A.D. Responses
series_description: Correspondence and documentation from jails requesting a deviation in reference to the jail construction/facility, or a variance request from an Ohio Minimum Jail Standard as it relates to the facility and operational practices; the Bureau''s response to the request.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in individual jail files until the facility is demolished.

series_description: Letters and other documentation as they relate to technical assistance requested and provided for local jails. Reports to include statistics, summaries of jail operations, comparisons involving one or more jails or BAD general operations.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain Five (5) years in individual jail files at regional and central office location

series_description: Worksheet completed during the annual jail inspection and updated annually as needed documenting elements that determine a jail''s Bureau Recommended Capacity (BRC) for holding and housing of prisoners.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until updated or the facility is destroyed. If the worksheet is updated, retain in the jail file at central office for One (1) year after the update.

series_description: Documentation of the book loans received from outside libraries for use by the inmate population.
retention_statement: Paper: Records to be maintained three years.

series_description: Documentation that Career-Technical teachers make industrial visits and have Advisory Committee meetings as required.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain records three years.

series_description: Monthly and Annual reports of educational programming. Includes Federal grant information (Special education, NCLB, Perkins, ABLE grants) as well as Tutor and Library Information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain seven years. ; Electronic: Retain seven years.

series_description: Lists of inmates waiting to be placed in various educational programs offered.
retention_statement: Paper: Record will be maintained on paper or electronically for one year. ; Electronic: Record will be maintained on paper or electronically for one year.

series_description: Teacher records and report cards of grades received in high school program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain records for three years. ; Electronic: Retain records for three years.

series_description: Courses of studies that are approved for teachers to use when instructing students in various groups.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superceded. ; Electronic: Retain until superceded.

series_description: Records include plans developed by teachers regarding the instructional objectives and methods that will be used to instruct classes.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain record for one year.

series_description: Documentation of the actual amount of hours the institutional libraries are in operation.
retention_statement: Paper: Record to be maintained three years.

series_description: Minutes from statewide and local apprenticeship meetings.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain record three years.

series_description: Documentation of Career-Technical Programs required for Ohio Department of Education Audits.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain records three years.

series_description: Files contain documentation needed for new teachers to get their five year education credentials. Also included is mentoring and licensure information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years in office. ; Electronic: Retain five years in office.

series_description: Records include documentation of who took the GED test including security of the test during that time.
retention_statement: Paper: Records to be maintained one year.

series_description: Document containing information on rules of the library and other pertinent documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Until superceded.

series_description: Information regarding the identification and program of inmates who receive special educational services. This includes Individualized Education Plan, and Multi-Factored Evaluation documentation as required by federal law.
retention_statement: Electronic: Any/all Special Education records to be maintained permanently. ; Paper: Retain at institution where inmate is/was housed at least until the end of incarceration. To be maintained on-site until transferred to off-site storage facility. Records to be maintained permanently in paper or electronic format. Paper records mayl be shredded if converted to electronic format.

series_description: Official notes from educational staff meetings.
retention_statement: Paper: Record will be maintained on paper or electronically for three years. ; Electronic: Record will be maintained on paper or electronically for three years.

series_description: Documentation of segregation admission screenings and determination of mental health risk assessments. File also includes documentation of segregation rounds.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_title: ACA Accreditation and Internal Management Audit Documents
series_description: Correspondence and reports relating to the American Correctional Association (ACA) accreditation. Includes Annual Certification Statements, Significant Incident Summaries and Notification, Outcome Measures Data, and Responses to Non-Compliance submitted to the Commission on Accreditation for Corrections, as well as all audit reports. This includes all Internal Management Audit reports, Responses to Non-Compliance and related documents as well.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office five years

series_description: Records indicating disposal of quantity and type of broken tools.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Record identifies each inmate assigned to an operation during the month and shows the amount of money the inmate was paid.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Hazardous and non-hazardous chemical lists and logs including DRC 1896 - Non-Hazardous Chemical Inventory Log, DRC 1886-Hazardous Chemical Log, DRC 1895- Chemical List, and the Facility List of Hazardous Chemicals.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Record documents emergency drill requirements per DRC Fire Safety Policy 10-SAF-05. DRC Form: Emergency Drill #1718.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_title: Exposure Assessment Report and Medical Evaluation
series_description: Record documents assessment of planned respirator use for DRC employees and inmates. This form also documents training of employees and inmates of the use of the selected respirator. Exposure Assessment Report #1701, Respirator Medical Evaluation 6325, and Respirator Training and Fit Testing Questionnaire 6060.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three years.

series_description: These forms are utilized to immediate safety issues only. DRC Forms included: Hot Work Permit #1517, Bicycle Inspection #1643, Confined Space Entry Permit/Lockout Tag Out #1682, Powered Industrial Truck Pre-Operation Log #6319, Confined Space Permit #6328, Respirator Inspection Record #6329.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one month from date of issue.

series_description: Description and/or investigation of staff and inmate injury. DRC Forms: #1796 Occupation Injury Report ,#4198 Inmate Accident Report,#6311 Inmate Accident Log, and other related documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain Five years ; Electronic: Retain Five years

series_description: Record to document analysis of employee and inmate job duties to assure proper safety process is in place. DRC Form: #1943 Job Hazard Analysis.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain ten years.

series_description: Hazardous and Infectious Waste records that document the disposal of hazardous waste materials and other documentation provided by contracted waste hauling companies that serve as shipping papers or hazardous waste manifest.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three years from the date waste was shipped for disposal.

series_description: An audit of computer hardware and software. This audit is performed by IT staff.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: This documentation is given to OPI customers for their input on product quality, delivery, customer satisfaction and overall customer service.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: This report provides a list of fixed assets for each specific OPI location.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Records used to perform annual inventory including count sheets, re-adjustment report, physical inventory report and total inventory value by account report.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Provide specific information related to any inmate accident that occurred in an OPI operation that required medical attention.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: This packet provides inmates assigned to an OPI operation specific shop rules including work schedule, evacuation plans, OPI pay scale, earned credit policy, health and safety checklist, and training checklist.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain while inmate is assigned to operation. Retain three years following reclass out of operation

series_description: Document provides a list of all training the inmate receives in the specific operation.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain while inmate is assigned to operation. Retain three years following reclass out of operation

series_title: OPI Central Office Table of Organization
series_description: Table of organization of OPI Executive staff.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: A list of fiscal accounts where OPI purchases are recorded.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Record provides each OPI operation the approved number of inmates that can be assigned to the operation and identifies the approved job titles and pay scale.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: A payroll report that tracks hours worked and calculates if required hours are met to be eligible to receive earned credit for a specific month.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Report given to institution cashier that provides total hours and total payroll amount for OPI operations.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: An annual quality assurance assessment of each operation to ensure quality procedures are in place and followed.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Record identifies and records specific training inmate received to handle hazardous materials.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain while inmate is assigned to operations. Retain three years followiing reclass out of operations

series_description: A document that describes the duties associated with a particular post. DRC Form #1326 - Post Orders Template (page1), DRC #1795 Poster Orders Template (page 2)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Documents the issue and return of DRC vehicle keys. DRC Form #1455
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Records the issuance and testing of personal body alarms. DRC Form #2252
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Documents involving the use of tools within the institution to include: DRC#2328 Lost, Missing, Stolen, or Broken Tool Report, #2329 Report of Contractor''s Tools, #2330 Tool Inventory Revision Report, #2331 Tool Inventory, #2332 Daily/Weekly Tool Report, #2552 Daily Tool Sign In/Out Log, #2671 Perpetual Inventory Log, and #5095 Syringe and Needle Count Sheet.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Rosters that record which officer is assigned to each post.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Log showing the date of receipt and delivery of legal mail to an inmate.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Computer generated record that records when fence alarms are triggered and then cleared.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain one year

series_description: Documentation that records an officer''s acknowledgement that the post orers have been read.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Records that indicate entrance and exit information for all vehicles and persons entering a vehicle sallyport. DRC Form #2477
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Documents the issue and return of equipment from the Control Center. DRC Form #1310
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Records telephone checks initiated by staff working after normal business hours.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Documents and tracks all contraband confiscated from inmates. DRC Form #6031
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Documents the daily security checks of the physical structure of the post area. DRC Form #2557
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Records the inspection of weapons. DRC Form #2448
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Records the issue and return of temporary key chits. DRC Form #2538
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Records the circumstances around which correction officer overtime is used. DRC Form #1096
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Documents the issue and return of cell phones belonging to the institution.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years.

series_description: Records inventories of equipment stored in the Control Center.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: A file documenting all packages received by offenders.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 10 years following final release, expiration of sentence, or death.

series_description: Documents inmate''s attendance to program meetings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year

series_title: Urine Test Results That Indicate No Illegal Drug Usage
series_description: Document results of inmate''s urine test that indicates no illegal drug use (negative results).
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy upon receipt.

series_description: Special Incident Reports - DRC Form #2091
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Routine and special incident reports to include: DRC#1000, Incident Report Form, and DRC#1001, Incident Report Supplement Form.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years ; Electronic: Retain three years

series_description: Documents involved in the review of use of force incidents to include: DRC#2693, DRC#2611, DRC#4181 and taped recordings of witness testimony.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_description: Agreements, memorandums of understanding, and correspondence relating to formal cooperation between DRC and other state agencies.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until agreement expires or is amended/terminated

series_description: Legal memorandums and correspondence containing analysis and advice on issues such as, but not limited to, pending legislation, juvenile issues, court decisions, statutory interpretation, human resource and inmate/supervision issues.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Correspondence related to requests for records of inmates under a sentence of death by the Capital Crimes Section of the Ohio Attorney General''s Office and the Ohio Public Defender.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years after inmate is executed or sentence commuted.

series_description: Files relating to inquiries/investigations/complaints received from U.S. Department of Labor, U.S. Department of Justice, IRS, and Ohio Public Employees Retirement System. Inquiries contain correspondence, complaints, audits and relevant information to the issue.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years after issue is decided and applicable appeals are decided

series_description: Correspondence related to international prison transfer requests from foreign nations and the U.S. Department of Justice.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office two years after decision is made on the transfer or the inmate is transferred. Retain an additional three years in Record Center

series_description: Electronic and paper copy case files of administrative claims assigned to special counsel, permanent total disability claims, fraud claims, violation of safety requirements, high dollar claims, and miscellaneous claims monitored in-house.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years after claim activity ends ; Electronic: Retain two years after claim activity ends

series_title: State Personnel Board of Review Case Files
series_description: Case records of appeals filed by employees or former employees and case records concerning other matters under the jurisdiction of and before the State Personnel Board of Review.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years after case is decided and appeals are exhausted

series_description: Data related to Central Office Publication Screening Committee''s submissions and decisions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain four years ; Electronic: Retains four years

series_description: Electronic record containing information relating to historical and current warrant entries, clears, and arrest data.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain permanently for reference material. This database is constantly being added to and never deleted. Information is historical record of warrants and violator-at-large periods and arrests.

series_description: Documents the collection, notification, and process for collection of court ordered obligation and payment for inmate cases.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until obligtion is paid and audited by internal auditors and report is released.

series_description: This form documents inmate''s Security Threat Group affiliation
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years after release from prison ; Electronic: Retain five years after release from prison

series_description: X-rays contain evaluation and diagnosis history that could be referenced as a result of the inmate returning to the penal system or if further treatment is needed upon inmate''s release.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain at institution until inmate is released. Upon release, maintain in storage for five years.

series_description: Names of substance, total quantity, finished form, number of units/volume of each finished form, number of commercial containers of each finished form, IF Scheduled I or II, the prescriber shall make exact count.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years ; Electronic: Retain three years

series_description: Labels of drugs repackaged by and stored within a pharmacy prior to being dispensed shall contain name of drug, identification of repackaged or by name or six digits of their terminal distributor of dangerous drugs.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years or one year past manufacturer''s expiration date, whichever is greater ; Electronic: Retain three years or one year past manufacturer''s expiration date, whichever is greater.

series_description: Record of all dangerous drugs received, administered, dispensed, distributed, sold, destroyed or used. Records shall include type, quantity, received name, address of person who received drug, name of person who administered, dispensed and used drug.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years ; Electronic: Retain three years

series_description: Records relating to practice of pharmacy and prescription records relating to practice of pharmacy.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years ; Electronic: Retain three years

series_description: All drugs purchased, quantity received, name address, distributor, registration or number; all drug orders, records of dispensed drugs - name, strength, quantity, date, patient name, positive identification of pharmacists, all records relating to practice of pharmacy, records of drugs dispensed to outpatients, records of drugs repackaged.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years ; Electronic: Retain three years

series_description: Cytological slides.
retention_statement: Electronic: Specimen is on a glass slide. Retain five yers

series_description: Patient test results other than blood bank and cytology.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain three years

series_description: Test systems QC records which helps Lab know if results are valid.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office two years, off-site storage one year ; Electronic: Retain three years

series_description: Scheduled instrument maintenance records required to keep in good working order.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office two years, off-site one year

series_description: Requisitions sent from Corrections Medical Center Lab customers.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years - one month in office, 2 years, eleven months off site

series_description: File contains correction officer application.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain file for 30 days from scheduled test date

series_description: Correction officer application file to include completed background investigation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years from the date assessed

series_description: File contains correction officer application to include assessment documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year from assessment date

series_description: File contains correction officer files that have been rejected.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain file two years from rejection date

series_description: File contains correction officer applications that have expired.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for six months from the date application was received

series_description: Documentation includes requests for mediation, mediations implemented, copies of agreements, mediator participations and dates of mediation. Separate database includes mediation, locations of mediation and results.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_description: Copies of all application forms and all pertinent documents, including request for payment form and DBA cover sheets. Database contains participant''s history in program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years ; Electronic: Retain two years

series_description: Copies of institution, central office, APA quarterly reports and sign-in sheets from various Health and Wellness events/trainings. Database contains program and participation history.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year ; Electronic: Retain one year

series_title: Decision of the Director on IPP (Intensive Program Prison) Termination
series_description: Institution documents supporting recommendation to the Director''''''''s designee that an inmate be removed from the program and where there is procedural error or no evidence to support the recommendation. File includes decision on inmmate status.
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy paper document after imaging. ; Electronic: Retain electronic images five years

series_description: Documents inmates training for safety related and equipment training items. DRC #1177-Safety Practice Acknowledgement Form and DRC #1953 - Inmate Training Form.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 10 (ten) years after expiration of sentence, final release, or death.

series_description: A log recording daily activities of employees and incarcerated persons that provides continuity of information from shift change to shift change.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years ; Electronic: Retain three years

series_description: Records relating to the maintenance, storage and use of firearms and other security devices stored in the institutional armory.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years

series_description: Report written by Adult Parole Authority Field Officers relating to offender''s supervision, criminal history and description of offense.
retention_statement: Electronic: 75 years.

series_description: Record conains information of inmate''s faith background, religious preference, and administer of record.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_title: Mental Health Services Institution and Central Office Directives
series_description: Documentation of program management directives that go beyond policy and standard operating procedures.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until replaced by revised directive.

series_description: Summary of medical somatic information in referrals, orders, tasks, etc., completed by mental health nursing staff and psychiatrist in an identified Institution which is submitted to Central Office.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_title: Rules Infraction Board Mental Health Assessment Log
series_description: Log that identifies offenders who have received a conduct report, the staff assigned, and the outcome.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years past date of Rules Infraction Board hearing ; Electronic: Retain three years past date of Rules Infraction Board hearing

series_description: Electronic database tracks residential treatment unit admissions and changes, medication changes, Oakwood Correctional Facility admissions, diagnosis, discharges, treatment form schedules, clinic and labs, MHL''s database for tracking of releases of information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain three years after offender is no longer in the residential treatment unit

series_description: Vitek procedure approvals, Ohio State Penitentiary admission referrals, uncontested transfers to Oakwood Correctional Facility and assessment for readmission to Oakwood Correctional Facility.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (currently audit cycle)

series_description: Documentation of medication dosages prescribed and received. Electronic database calculates percentage of medication compliance. Results are printed from program and placed in offender''s file.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years ; Electronic: Retain three years

series_description: Access based computerized treatment planner used in all institutions.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain three years after offender is released from prison

series_description: Records track individuals and group information from admission to suicide watch status and monitors suicide watch follow-up for five years.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain fifteen years

series_description: Managers and clinicial supervisors maintain records of ongoing supervision plans. Information includes supervision documentation for licensure supervision and extensions of license to supervised staff.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years beyond staff''s employment in institution

series_description: Mental Health managers are required to complete and submit to Central Office contract monitoring forms.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years beyond use of contractor services

series_description: Log documents staff''s assignment and completion of transfers and discharge summaries.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain five years

series_title: Release and Reentry Program Referral Log
series_description: Log contains Release Preparation, Community Linkage, Halfway House summaries, Path and ACT information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years after offender leaves institution
Paper: Retain three years ; Electronic: Retain three years

series_description: Database of inmate death that includes date and time of death, cause of death, race, age, gender, location and brief description.
retention_statement: Paper: Permanent ; Electronic: Permanent

series_description: An individual interested in applying for clemency must request an application in writing.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until application packet is mailed.

series_description: Lab results for orders pertaining to blood grouping or crossmatch or antibody screens.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain ten years on database.

series_description: Identifies the direction and method that OPI is planning to market their products and services.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Document provides a collective overview of all OPI operations and services. This report reflects total sales and total profit for OPI.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years

series_description: This report provides information on prison year status of operation, fiscal year-end amounts, and critical issues.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain ten years, review with State before destroying.

series_title: Bureau of Sentence Computation Legal File
series_description: An accurate and complete record pertaining to the calculation of an inmate''s sentence which includes copies of official court documents and any other related papers created to document the time an inmae is required to serve with the Department of Rehabilitation and Correction.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for ten years after death, final release, maximum expiration or definite sentence expiration

series_description: A copy or any conduct report authorized by an employee that is maintained in the employee''s RIB file.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Records placement of inmates in Security, Discipline and Local Control.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current ACA audit cycle)

series_description: Records placement of inmates in Level 4 and 5 security status.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current ACA audit cycle)

series_description: Inventory system that retains the data of the inventory stored at each facility including the quantity, cost, billing information, receiving report and vendor.
retention_statement: Electronic: Until audited.

series_description: Contains images of inmates and offenders including profiles of identifying scars, marks, and tattoos.
retention_statement: Electronic: 99 years

series_title: Bureau of Mental Health Communication Documents
series_description: Includes bimonthly mental health leadership meeting notes, clinical notes newsletter, monthly central office meeting notes and social security reports.
retention_statement: Electronic: 15 years ; Paper: 15 years

series_description: Includes nonformulary medication approvals and involuntary medication hearings.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 year audit cycle

series_title: Request for Mental Health Services from the Adult Parole Authority
series_description: Form used to request clinical risk assessments, treatment summaries, and capacity assessments.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years

series_description: Log used to record all verbal or written requests for mental health services from offenders, staff, or the community. Log contains assignment, completion, and outcome notation.
retention_statement: Electronic: 5 years ; Paper: 5 years

series_description: Includes mental health external review reports, technical assistance reports, institution consultation and teleconference notes.
retention_statement: Electronic: 10 years

series_title: Court of Claims Case File Database
series_description: Local electronic program containing detailed information on cases filed against ODRC in the Court of Claims of Ohio.
retention_statement: Electronic: 50 years.

series_description: Each institution generates communications to other departments, agencies, regarding inmate deaths, legislative inquiries and Inspection Committees.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years
