series_description: File of offender letters and Bureau of Mental Health Service response. File also includes copy of response to mental health manager.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain ten years after death, final release or expiration of sentence

series_description: Files and records of courses and activities staff engage in to renew their professional credentials. Records are maintained by the Local Professional Development Committee and the Superintendent''s designee.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superceded. ; Electronic: Retain until superceded.

series_description: Record of topics and issues discussed during staff meeting.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: This file contains all information on sex offenders from the point they enter the Sex Offender Risk Reduction Center (SORCC) at the Madison Correctional Institution during reception. All information regarding sex offender assessment, programming and treatment are contained in the file.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until offender completes the SORCC program ; Electronic: Retain electronically for 10 years following offender''s final release, maximum expiration or death

series_description: Database that documents all admission mental health files were reviewed and flagged for intellectual disability testing, completion, and follow-up tracking.
retention_statement: Electronic: 3 years following the release of the offender

series_description: Contains records of Violation Sanctions Process (VSP) hearings including the Notice of Findings, Sanction Receipt, Revocation Order, Hearing Data Sheet and Hearing Officer Report.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper file pending Post Revocation Hearing, if applicable ; Electronic: Retain for ten years after death, final release, maximum expiration of sentence

series_description: Database for the Professional Alliance of Correctional Employees that includes applications by location and total number of applications received annually. These records include name, location, current position and desired mentoring opportunity, completion stats, reason for not completing program, mentor information, and training stats.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Records the Prison Administrative Management (PAM) weekly rounds within the institution to ensure that quality of life issues are being met and encourages communication with staff and inmates.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (currently ACA audit cycle)

series_description: Local electronic program includes detail information on cases filed in the Federal and State courts (excluding Court of Claims of Ohio).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for reference material. This databse is constantly being added to and never removed from.

series_description: Local electronic program containing information pertaining to individual worker''s compensation claims.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for reference material. This databse is constantly added to and never removed from.

series_description: Reports produced from within the Department of Rehabilitation and Correction that contain data routinely requested by the public and/or universally used throughout the department. Policy 01-COM-12.
retention_statement: Paper: Permanent ; Electronic: An electronic pdf placed on DRC website

series_description: Records that pertain to an inmate''s participation in Apprenticeship programming. This includes master record and hourse worked in the field.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain at institution where inmate is/was housed at least until the end of incarceration. Maintain permanently at off-site storage.

series_title: Education Management File (formerly Academic and Vocational Records)
series_description: Record of inmate participation in educational programming including Academics, Career-Technical, Post Secondary and Appreticeship. Includes the transcript of all such activities. Also inlcudes education counseling information.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain records at institution where incarcerated. Maintain permanently off-site transcript, high school information, and career/technical completion documentation.

series_description: Database that contains inmate''''''''s trust fund, personal account transactions including debt and collection orders.
retention_statement: Electronic: 99 years

series_description: Documents maintained in preparation for an offender''s release, which may include monthly release preparation reports, workshop attendance sheets, exemption forms, and release preparation program agendas.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain for one audit cycle (one year)

series_description: The official record of the decisions of the parole board.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain electronically ten years after death, final release, maximum expiration or definite sentence expiration ; Paper: Retain in office two years.

series_description: An accurate and complete record of all Parole Board Hearings including Decision Sheets, Risk Assessments, Institutional Summary Reports, Parole Candidate Information Sheets, Confidential Sheets, Clinical Risk Assessment (CRA), Transitional Control Screening, Projected Release Date Reviews, Parole Violator Recommission/Kellogg Review, Kellogg Mitigation Hearing Digest, Vote Sheets, and Sexually Violent Predator Review and/or Hearing.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for ten years after death, final release, maximum expiration or definite sentence expiration ; Paper: Retain in file pending a final action and issuance of minutes

series_description: The primary application platform for the department and is the offender tracking system during incarceration, parole and/or probation. This application manages offender information from admission to final release from supervision.
retention_statement: Electronic: 99 years

series_description: Database that contains Sugarcreek Development Unit census, referrals, caseload, and discharge tracking.
retention_statement: Electronic: 99 years

series_description: DRC Form 2161 that lists persons authorized to enter the institution with photo identification.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 years

series_description: Video tape of events leading up to and following a cell extraction.
retention_statement: Electronic: 3 years unless the event is involved in litigation

series_description: Minutes and supporting records documenting agency meetings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years. ; Electronic: Retain 3 years.

series_description: Audio recordings of Rules Infraction Board (R.I.B.) hearings.
retention_statement: Electronic: 3 years

series_description: Minutes of the Prison Labor Advisory Committee meetings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years (current audit cycle)

series_description: Community service documentation that may include the application, commnity service hours tracking, work-site plan, work-site inspection, and community service survey.
retention_statement: Paper: 2 years after completion of community service project

series_description: Contains records from the Human Subjects Research Review process for each application to conduct research involving inmates, staff, and/or department records. Each file contains the following Application for Review of Research Proposal (DRC 1836), Research Proposal Approval (DRC 1827), communcation with researchers and staff, and completed research papers.
retention_statement: Paper: 4 years after completion of the research

series_title: Grant Awards Related to Offender Workforce Development
series_description: Request to Purchase documentation, invoices, participant information, curricula.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain 5 years.

series_description: E-mail and letter requests for Federal Bonding Program information and employment assistance.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain paper copies 1 year. ; Electronic: Maintain subsequent copies electronically 1 year.

series_title: Offender Workforce Development Videoconference Schedules and Resumes
series_description: Packeted information regarding each scheduled Offender Workforce Development Videoconference. Includes institutional schedule, submitted resumes and detail sheets (electronic form).
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain 2 years. ; Electronic: Maintain electronic records 2 years.

series_title: Offender Workforce Development Videoconference Participation Database
series_description: Database by annual institutional participation of inmates submitted and participating in Offender Workforce Development Videoconferences.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain 3 years

series_description: Electronic resources of employers known to hire ex-offenders. This database is an internal document not meant for public dissemination. It is no longer used by Offender Workforce Development Administrators and has not been updated in approximately 18 months.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain 1 year.

series_description: Electronic database of bonds issued: Includes employer information, employee information including SSN and address, bond issued and date. Federal Bonding applications: includes telephone application, copy of bonding certificate, and employer notification letter. Not processed for reasons of failure to complete application process, non-conformation of hire. Federal Bonding applications: includes telephone application, copy of bonding certificate, and employer notification letter. Completed packets are maintained electronically.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain 2 years.

series_title: Bureau of Classification Protective Custody Records
series_description: Documentation that includes protective custody requests, removals, appeals, and/or transfers in lieu of protective custody.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain three years. ; Electronic: Maintain three years.

series_description: Checklists, logs, and reports used in the institutional food services. Includes but is not limited to DRC Form 1779, 2302, 5274, 1595, 1207, 1495, 1256, and 1177.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years.

series_description: An accurate and complete record of all information pertaining to an inmate with a Sexually Violent Predator (SVP) designation including Parole Board SVP Reviews and SVP Hearings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until a decision is made by the Parole Board to terminate control over the inmate''s service of prison term to include issuance of parole board minutes. ; Electronic: Retain for 10 years after death, final release, maximum expiration or defeinite sentence.

series_description: Audit reports completed by Bureau of Community Sanctions'' staff regarding program performance/standard compliance of DRC funded community correction programs.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years

series_title: Offender Job Linkage Historical Reports and Program Summaries
series_description: Program information summaries and statistics compiled for presentations and reports as requested.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain 5 years.

series_title: Bureau of Classification Institutional Count Records
series_description: Records that document the weekly count of the correctional institutions.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain five years.

series_description: Hazard Communication Program that describes how the institution plans to ensure that the hazardous materials used at the worksite are properly evaluated, appropriately labeled, the location of the MSDS, and how the institution will provide hazard information training.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until supersheded

series_description: A written plan which provides for preventative measures for the storage of combustible materials, good housekeeping measures, maintenance of fire protection, detection and extinguishing equipment, and provides for the prevention of fire hazards.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded

series_description: A log used to document the testing and ispection of fire extinguishing equipment.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 years ; Electronic: 3 years

series_description: Records of the facility annual fire inspections and documented corrective action.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 years

series_description: DRC Form 1701 used to document the respiratory hazard identification and analysis.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 3 years.

series_description: A written plan that provides guidance on basic facility housekeeping tasks including the frequency of the task, materials needed for the task and genreal instructions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded

series_title: OSHA Work-Related Injuries and Illness Log, Illness and Injury Report, and Summary
series_description: OSHA Forms 300, 300A and 301 used to document, classify and summarize work-related injuries and illnesses.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years.

series_description: A document containing information and istruction on hazardous materials present in the workplace. Material Safety Data Sheets (MSDS)contain details about hazards, requirements for its safe handling, and actions to be taken in the event of fire, spill or over-exposure.
retention_statement: Electronic: 30 years ; Paper: 30 years

series_description: Records the annual food service inspection conducted by the Department of Health and documented corrective action.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 years

series_title: Public Employees Risk Reduction Program Inspection Reports
series_description: Documentation of Public Employees Risk Reduction Program (PERRP) inspections, reports, and abatements.
retention_statement: Electronic: 5 years ; Paper: 5 years

series_description: A medical questionaire completed by anyone performing duties requiring the use of a respirator.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until next evaluation

series_description: Written respiratory protection program with work-site specific procedures with standard operating procedures governing the selection and use of respirators.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded

series_description: Documents the testing required to ensure an approved respirator is properly fitted.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until next fit test

series_description: A written policy and procedure developed and approved by an authorized appointing authority other than the Dirctor. Written policies and procedures will define operational guidelines for facility employees, volunteers, inmate and the public.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years after policy is superseded, obsolete, or replaced. The State should review the records before they are disposed.

series_description: Safety Manual that documents the health and safety programs and procedures specific to the facility that promotes a safe and healthful workplace.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded

series_description: Documents the entire investigation process, including the sexual abuse investigations, desccribing the facts found while conduting an investigatio invlving an employee or offender''s alleged violation of policy, procedure, or Ohio Revised Code.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain at facility 10 years after the involved inmate(s) has achieved final release or death ; Electronic: Retain at facility 10 years after the involved inmate(s) has achieved final release or death
